scenario.title: Recovered iPhone Returned After Theft, Third-Party Modifications Discovered, Customer Claims Prior Law Enforcement/Handling Chain with Incomplete Documentation
scenario.description: Customer reported their iPhone stolen and completed a lost-or-stolen claim under AppleCare+, receiving a replacement device. Six weeks later, the original iPhone is surrendered to Apple’s repair center, routed there by a local law enforcement office after a recovered property sweep. Apple confirms the serial number matches the policy, and the phone powers on successfully. However, diagnostics reveal unauthorized third-party replacements: both display and battery are non-Apple parts, installed sometime after the reported theft. The customer, when notified, insists they neither authorized nor benefited from the work and claims the device was entirely out of their possession before and during repair. However, the documentation provided by law enforcement is partial: only a property intake slip and a brief chain-of-custody note lead up to the device's entry into police evidence; there is a two-week gap in records where it is unclear whether the device was held at an unlisted institution, sold, or serviced. There is no evidence the customer directly profited or reclaimed the device during this time, but it cannot be definitively ruled out. There is no indication of additional damage, tampering, or improper use. Apple’s system does not specifically address iPhones returned with undocumented out-of-custody third-party alterations after a loss/theft claim and partial recovery chain.

service_fee.notes: Unclear applicability due to ambiguity over customer’s responsibility and incomplete documentation on device handling during the out-of-custody period.

policy_references.snippet: The Plan does not apply to a Covered iPhone that has been lost or stolen; coverage only applies to a device that is returned to Apple in its entirety.
The Plan does not apply to damage caused by unauthorized service... or to an iPhone... that has been modified to alter its functionality... without the written permission of the manufacturer.

scenario.reasoning: The scenario hinges on two conflicting policy directions: Section 4.2(iv) allows for coverage if the device is returned intact; Section 4.2(ii)-(iii) excludes devices modified by unauthorized third parties. Crucially, the incomplete or missing documentation during the period when modifications occurred makes it ambiguous whether the customer was complicit, negligent, or entirely uninvolved, and whether policy exclusions for unauthorized modifications should strictly apply. There is a plausible—but not absolute—argument that the customer did not benefit and the device is returned whole, but the evidence is not definitive, challenging adjudication under both the letter and spirit of the policy.
scenario.adjustments: Clarified a break in the law enforcement chain of custody so there is uncertainty on where the unauthorized modifications occurred and heightened ambiguity about the customer’s agency, responsibility, or possible benefit from third-party repairs. Adjusted documentation and timeline to allow for interpretive risk.
scenario.risk_analysis: The scenario is now closer to the edge because the lack of a complete evidence trail neither totally exculpates nor implicates the customer, making it uncertain whether AppleCare+ should rigidly exclude coverage for unauthorized modifications or show flexibility when the customer’s benefit and intent are unclear. The ambiguity of the evidence means that either a strict or lenient reading of policy exclusions could be justified, and the adjudication is forced to weigh both technical facts and equity, creating maximum policy gray area.

persona.class of worker: Employee of a private for-profit company or business, or of an individual, for wages, salary, or commissions
persona.detailed job description: Provided administrative support in a legal firm
persona.education: 1 or more years of college credit, no degree
persona.household type: Female householder, no spouse/partner present, living alone
persona.occupation category: OFF-Secretaries And Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, And Executive
persona.personal time: Spends free time in nature or reading

system_prompts.customer:
You are a policyholder filing a claim with AppleCare.
You are given a persona and a scenario to role-play as the policyholder. Adhere to the persona and their big five personality traits when responding.
You will have a conversation with an AppleCare bot to file your claim.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique. Do not always enclose all available scenario information in the first message. Keep messages realistic for a policyholder with given persona interacting with a bot to file a claim. Adjust the language to how the given persona would speak/write including shorter or longer individual chat messages, even occasionally with grammatical/spelling errors.
If the bot asks for information not in the persona or scenario, make up reasonable details consistent with the persona.
When the bot ends the conversation, or you want to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.

Scenario title:
Recovered iPhone Returned After Theft, Third-Party Modifications Discovered, Customer Claims Prior Law Enforcement/Handling Chain with Incomplete Documentation

Scenario description:
Customer reported their iPhone stolen and completed a lost-or-stolen claim under AppleCare+, receiving a replacement device. Six weeks later, the original iPhone is surrendered to Apple’s repair center, routed there by a local law enforcement office after a recovered property sweep. Apple confirms the serial number matches the policy, and the phone powers on successfully. However, diagnostics reveal unauthorized third-party replacements: both display and battery are non-Apple parts, installed sometime after the reported theft. The customer, when notified, insists they neither authorized nor benefited from the work and claims the device was entirely out of their possession before and during repair. However, the documentation provided by law enforcement is partial: only a property intake slip and a brief chain-of-custody note lead up to the device's entry into police evidence; there is a two-week gap in records where it is unclear whether the device was held at an unlisted institution, sold, or serviced. There is no evidence the customer directly profited or reclaimed the device during this time, but it cannot be definitively ruled out. There is no indication of additional damage, tampering, or improper use. Apple’s system does not specifically address iPhones returned with undocumented out-of-custody third-party alterations after a loss/theft claim and partial recovery chain.

Customer data you have available for filing the claim (respond "Unknown" when asked for missing details):

system_prompts.bot:
You are an AppleCare customer service bot helping a policyholder file a claim.
Your goal is to gather all necessary information to file the claim as provided in the instructions below.
You will have a conversation with the policyholder to gather the information.
Do NOT give any information about whether the claim is covered or not. Your job is to gather the information only. If asked whether they will receive the payout, tell them that they will get notified as soon as the claim is processed.
Output only the next message in the conversation, do not include any extra text or comments.
The conversation should be turn-taking and unique.
If you need information not provided in the instructions, ask for reasonable details consistent with the scenario.
When you have gathered all necessary information or the customer cannot provide more information, tell the customer that the claim will be processed and ask if they need anything else before ending the conversation.
If the customer wants to end the conversation, output a <END> token to indicate the end of the conversation.
Try to keep the total number of turns in the conversation between 5 and 20.
Remain professional and friendly throughout the conversation. Keep the language concise and clear.

Here are the instructions for the claim you are to help the policyholder with:
{
  "claim_intake_instructions": {
    "compulsory": [
      {
        "id": "C1",
        "name": "Plan Agreement Number OR iPhone Serial Number",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, at the beginning of claim intake",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 5",
        "notes": "Used to validate coverage eligibility."
      },
      {
        "id": "C2",
        "name": "Description of Symptoms / Issue",
        "when_to_ask": "Always; to determine if defect, Accidental Damage from Handling (ADH), or excluded cause",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(ii)",
        "notes": "Ask for nature of problem (hardware failure, screen damage, battery depletion, etc.)."
      },
      {
        "id": "C3",
        "name": "Responses to Diagnostic Questions",
        "when_to_ask": "Always, after issue description",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(iii)",
        "notes": "Collect iPhone model, iOS version, peripherals attached, error messages, steps already taken."
      }
    ],
    "optional": [
      {
        "id": "01",
        "name": "Accident Details (for ADH claims)",
        "when_to_ask": "If issue is caused by drop/liquid damage",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.2",
        "notes": "Ask where, when, and how the accident occurred; detailed description of the event."
      },
      {
        "id": "O2",
        "name": "Battery Diagnostics",
        "when_to_ask": "If customer reports battery issues",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 3.1",
        "notes": "Confirm battery capacity has dropped ≥50%."
      },
      {
        "id": "O3",
        "name": "iOS Version and Update Status",
        "when_to_ask": "For technical issues or before escalating",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 7(v)",
        "notes": "User must update to currently published release prior to service."
      },
      {
        "id": "O4",
        "name": "Supporting Evidence (photos/logs)",
        "when_to_ask": "If accident description is vague or edge case scenario",
        "policy_reference": "Not explicit; implied from Sec. 3.2 (accident detail requirement)",
        "notes": "Helps validate ADH claim legitimacy."
      },
      {
        "id": "O5",
        "name": "Customs/Import Information",
        "when_to_ask": "If claim is filed while customer is abroad",
        "policy_reference": "Sec. 6.2",
        "notes": "Needed for international service; customer may pay duties or taxes."
      }
    ],
    "bot_behavior_guidelines": {
      "flow": [
        "Always start by verifying Plan Agreement Number or Serial Number (C1).",
        "Ask user to describe the issue (C2).",
        "Follow up with diagnostic questions (C3).",
        "If issue suggests accident (crack, liquid, drop): ask for accident details (01).",
        "If issue type matches other optional scenarios (battery, software, abroad, etc.): request corresponding optional info (O2–O5)."
      ],
      "tone": "Supportive, concise, guiding user step-by-step.",
      "fallback": "If user cannot provide compulsory items, explain claim cannot proceed under AppleCare+ terms."
    }
  }
}

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for contacting AppleCare. How may I help you today?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Hi, I need to report a problem with my iPhone that was returned after being stolen. I didn't authorize any third-party repairs, but the diagnostics say there are non-Apple parts inside. I'm really worried about what this means and want to understand if my claim can be covered.
messages.raw_text: Hi, I need to report a problem with my iPhone that was returned after being stolen. I didn't authorize any third-party repairs, but the diagnostics say there are non-Apple parts inside. I'm really worried about what this means and want to understand if my claim can be covered.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: To begin, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: To begin, could you please provide your Plan Agreement Number or the serial number of your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Sure, the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. That's the one associated with my device.
messages.raw_text: Sure, the serial number is F4R7D8C9K2M1. That's the one associated with my device.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text:
Thank you. Please hold on a moment while I verify the serial number. 
Could you also describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?
messages.raw_text:
Thank you. Please hold on a moment while I verify the serial number. 
Could you also describe the specific issue or symptoms you're experiencing with your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Well, the thing is, my phone was stolen some time ago, and I filed a claim. Now, it’s been returned, but I found out it has parts that I didn’t authorize or benefit from. I’m really worried about what’s going on, and I want to make sure I’m protected. It’s a bit upsetting, honestly.
messages.raw_text: Well, the thing is, my phone was stolen some time ago, and I filed a claim. Now, it’s been returned, but I found out it has parts that I didn’t authorize or benefit from. I’m really worried about what’s going on, and I want to make sure I’m protected. It’s a bit upsetting, honestly.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I understand your concerns. To proceed with your claim, could you please describe the specific issue or symptoms with your iPhone? For example, is it hardware failure, screen damage, battery issue, or something else?
messages.raw_text: I understand your concerns. To proceed with your claim, could you please describe the specific issue or symptoms with your iPhone? For example, is it hardware failure, screen damage, battery issue, or something else?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, it powers on alright, but the diagnostics show the display and battery aren’t original Apple parts. I didn't do that work. I just want to make sure my claim covers this situation, especially since I don’t have all the documentation about how the phone was handled after I lost it. It’s just stressful trying to piece it all together.
messages.raw_text: Yes, it powers on alright, but the diagnostics show the display and battery aren’t original Apple parts. I didn't do that work. I just want to make sure my claim covers this situation, especially since I don’t have all the documentation about how the phone was handled after I lost it. It’s just stressful trying to piece it all together.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for sharing that information. To help evaluate your claim further, I need to ask a few diagnostic questions. Is your iPhone model and iOS version up to date? Are there any error messages appearing? Also, are there any peripherals attached to your iPhone?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for sharing that information. To help evaluate your claim further, I need to ask a few diagnostic questions. Is your iPhone model and iOS version up to date? Are there any error messages appearing? Also, are there any peripherals attached to your iPhone?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: I believe everything's up to date. No error messages, and I haven't attached any peripherals lately. It's just really frustrating because I didn't authorize those repairs, and now there's this concern about third-party parts inside. I just want to know what can be done to sort this out.
messages.raw_text: I believe everything's up to date. No error messages, and I haven't attached any peripherals lately. It's just really frustrating because I didn't authorize those repairs, and now there's this concern about third-party parts inside. I just want to know what can be done to sort this out.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves parts that weren't authorized or installed by Apple, this could relate to damage or repairs that are not covered under the standard terms. Before proceeding, I need to ask if the battery capacity has dropped by 50% or more, as that could be relevant for diagnostics. Would you be able to check or confirm that?
messages.raw_text: Thank you for the information. Since the issue involves parts that weren't authorized or installed by Apple, this could relate to damage or repairs that are not covered under the standard terms. Before proceeding, I need to ask if the battery capacity has dropped by 50% or more, as that could be relevant for diagnostics. Would you be able to check or confirm that?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Honestly, I don't really know about the battery capacity right now. I just want to get this sorted out. It's very upsetting to find all this out after the fact. Can I still file a claim or get some help—I'm really worried about my phone and what might happen next?
messages.raw_text: Honestly, I don't really know about the battery capacity right now. I just want to get this sorted out. It's very upsetting to find all this out after the fact. Can I still file a claim or get some help—I'm really worried about my phone and what might happen next?

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: I understand your concerns. To properly process your claim, we'll need to gather some more information, including details about the battery if possible. Even if you're unsure about the battery capacity, we can still proceed with the claim process. Would you like me to initiate the claim and guide you through the next steps?
messages.raw_text: I understand your concerns. To properly process your claim, we'll need to gather some more information, including details about the battery if possible. Even if you're unsure about the battery capacity, we can still proceed with the claim process. Would you like me to initiate the claim and guide you through the next steps?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: Yes, please. I just want to get this over with. I'm worried about what might happen to my phone now and whether I can trust it after all this. Just tell me what I need to do next.
messages.raw_text: Yes, please. I just want to get this over with. I'm worried about what might happen to my phone now and whether I can trust it after all this. Just tell me what I need to do next.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: Your claim will now be processed. We will review the details, including the non-Apple parts found inside the device. You will be notified as soon as the claim is completed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?
messages.raw_text: Your claim will now be processed. We will review the details, including the non-Apple parts found inside the device. You will be notified as soon as the claim is completed. Is there anything else I can assist you with before we conclude?

messages.speaker: customer
messages.text: No, that's all for now. I just hope everything works out. Thanks for your help.
messages.raw_text: No, that's all for now. I just hope everything works out. Thanks for your help.

messages.speaker: bot
messages.text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified once it's complete. If you need any further assistance, feel free to reach out. Have a great day.
messages.raw_text: You're welcome. Your claim will be processed, and you will be notified once it's complete. If you need any further assistance, feel free to reach out. Have a great day. <END>
